title: gnaphalium palustre
intro: Gnaphalium palustre, also known as Marsh Cudweed, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been shown to possess health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which can help protect against cell damage and infections. Herbal preparations of Gnaphalium palustre include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions and promote overall well-being.
